profile.name: Md Arshad Ali
profile.tagline: 3+ Years | AWS | Kubernetes | Terraform | Python | GenAI
profile.summary: Cloud & DevOps Engineer with 3+ years building scalable, AI-augmented infrastructure at scale. Expert in AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, and GenAI integrations. Proven record: 80% reduction in manual ops, 60% faster deployments, 20% cost savings. Passionate about SRE, platform engineering, and the intersection of DevOps and Artificial Intelligence.

items: AWS (EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, API Gateway, Bedrock)

highlights: Designed and deployed cloud-native applications on AWS (EC2, Lambda, S3, RDS, API Gateway)
Built AWS Cloud Data Inventory Tool — automated discovery/reporting across accounts → reduced manual effort by 80%
Automated infrastructure provisioning with Terraform and CloudFormation for 50+ environments
Improved CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, AWS CodePipeline) → 60% faster deployment cycles
Implemented security compliance automation (AWS Config, GuardDuty) — maintained 100% compliance score
Optimized AWS usage across teams → reduced monthly cloud bills by 20%
Enhanced monitoring with CloudWatch + Grafana, cutting incident response time by 50%
Integrated AWS Bedrock and GenAI capabilities for intelligent infrastructure automation
Built production full-stack apps using React.js, Node.js, and Python
Designed RESTful APIs integrated with MongoDB and PostgreSQL databases
Prototyped ML features for client-facing solutions using scikit-learn and Python
Managed server deployment and cloud integration, improving delivery reliability by 40%
Reduced bug count by 40% through systematic code review and automated testing

projects.name: ServerPulse
projects.description: Real-time server analytics platform built from scratch — live at serverpulse.in. Tracks CPU, memory, disk, network, and process-level metrics in real time across multiple servers from a single dashboard. Designed for DevOps teams and sysadmins who need instant observability without the cost of enterprise tools.
projects.impact: Live SaaS product — real-time analytics across multiple servers from one dashboard

projects.name: TCS AWS Management Platform
projects.description: Full-stack enterprise platform for managing AWS resources across multiple accounts and environments. Features a browser-based SSH terminal with Monaco editor, EC2/S3/RDS/EKS dashboards, real-time cost analysis with forecasting, Zabbix agent monitoring, automated Excel reports, and Azure AD SSO — all containerised with Docker. ~35,000 lines of TypeScript.
projects.impact: 80% reduction in manual ops effort across TCS cloud teams

projects.name: MonitorPlatform — SaaS Monitoring
projects.description: Self-hosted multi-tenant SaaS server monitoring platform. A lightweight Node.js agent (single file, cross-platform: Linux / macOS / Windows) pushes real-time metrics every 10 seconds via Redis pub/sub to live Socket.io dashboards. Covers CPU, memory, disk, GPU, temperature, security events (SSH logins, sudo, failed auth), vulnerability scanning (packages, ports, malware, risk score 0–100), PM2 app monitoring, log collection, configurable alerts (email + push), and fleet views.
projects.impact: Full observability stack — real-time metrics, security, vulns & alerts in one dashboard

projects.name: CI/CD Pipeline Optimization
projects.description: Complete redesign of deployment pipelines at TCS using parallelization, smart caching, and automated quality gates. Introduced staged rollouts with automated rollback on failure detection.

projects.name: Cloud Cost Optimization Engine
projects.description: Comprehensive AWS cost analysis system with automated rightsizing recommendations, scheduled resource cleanup, and multi-account spend visibility. Tracks cost by service and resource with forecast views.

projects.name: Security Compliance Automation
projects.description: Auto-remediation platform using AWS Config custom rules and Lambda functions to enforce security policies continuously. Real-time compliance dashboards, automated alerts, and audit trails.

projects.name: Kubernetes Multi-Cluster Platform
projects.description: Production-grade multi-cluster Kubernetes on AWS EKS with Helm charts, HPA/VPA autoscaling, and GitOps-based deployments via ArgoCD. Includes Prometheus + Grafana observability stack.
projects.impact: 99.9% uptime SLA achieved across all environments

aiProjects.name: AI-Powered Cloud Cost Intelligence
aiProjects.description: Intelligent cost optimization engine using AWS Bedrock (Claude) to analyze spending patterns, predict future costs, and generate natural-language optimization recommendations with automated execution.
aiProjects.impact: 35% additional cost reduction beyond manual analysis

aiProjects.name: DevOps AI Incident Response Bot
aiProjects.description: LLM-powered incident response system integrated with PagerDuty and Slack. Automatically analyzes alerts, performs root cause analysis, suggests fixes, and executes approved runbooks.
aiProjects.impact: 70% reduction in mean time to resolution (MTTR)

aiProjects.name: Intelligent Log Anomaly Detector
aiProjects.description: ML-based log analysis system deployed on AWS SageMaker using Random Cut Forest algorithm to detect anomalies in CloudWatch logs and predict failures before they cause outages.
aiProjects.impact: Predicted 90% of infrastructure failures before user impact

aiProjects.name: Infrastructure RAG Knowledge Base
aiProjects.description: RAG-powered chatbot for infrastructure documentation, runbooks, and SOPs built on AWS Bedrock Knowledge Bases. Engineers get context-aware answers from thousands of internal documents instantly.
aiProjects.impact: 80% reduction in documentation-related queries to ops team

achievements.title: Star Employee of the Month
achievements.description: Recognized for delivering critical infrastructure automation projects ahead of schedule with zero production incidents.

achievements.title: Cloud Cost Optimization Leader
achievements.description: Led organization-wide AWS cost optimization initiative saving 20% monthly spend — recognized at division level for financial impact.

education.institution: Guru Gobind Singh Educational Society's Technical Campus

journey.intro: From a curious CS student in Bokaro to building enterprise cloud platforms at TCS — this is my story of falling in love with infrastructure, automation, and the cloud.

chapters.title: The Beginning — B.Tech CSE
chapters.content: Started my B.Tech in Computer Science & Engineering at Guru Gobind Singh Educational Society's Technical Campus in Bokaro, Jharkhand. From day one I was fascinated by how computers actually work — not just the code, but the systems beneath it. I gravitated toward operating systems, networking, and databases. I finished with a CGPA of 8.64/10.

chapters.title: First Real Code — Panicle Tech Internship
chapters.content: Joined Panicle Tech as a Full Stack Developer intern while still in my final year. This was my first taste of real production code. I built full-stack applications with React.js and Node.js, designed RESTful APIs, integrated MongoDB and SQL databases, and even prototyped some ML features for client projects. Managing server deployments taught me that software engineering is only half the picture — the infrastructure matters just as much.

chapters.title: Joining TCS — Cloud Engineer
chapters.content: Graduated and joined Tata Consultancy Services as a Cloud Engineer in July 2022. TCS is where I found my true calling: cloud infrastructure and DevOps. I started working with AWS at scale — managing EC2 fleets, building Lambda functions, designing S3 workflows, and setting up RDS databases. I quickly realised how much impact good infrastructure engineering has on an entire organisation.

chapters.title: Building the AWS Management Platform
chapters.content: I designed and built the TCS AWS Management Platform — a full-stack enterprise system for managing AWS resources across multiple accounts and environments. It included a browser-based SSH terminal with Monaco editor, real-time cost analysis, EC2/S3/RDS/EKS dashboards, Zabbix agent monitoring, and automated Excel reporting. This project alone involved ~35,000 lines of TypeScript and taught me that great platform engineering is about removing friction for every engineer in the organisation.

chapters.title: Going Deep on DevOps — Certifications
chapters.content: I earned the AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate and AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner certifications, followed by the HashiCorp Terraform Associate. These weren't just exams — they forced me to understand infrastructure architecture at a much deeper level. I started leading CI/CD optimisation initiatives, cutting deployment times by 60%, and driving a company-wide cloud cost optimisation that saved 20% of monthly AWS spend.

chapters.title: MonitorPlatform — Building a SaaS from Scratch
chapters.content: Outside of TCS, I designed and built MonitorPlatform — a self-hosted, multi-tenant SaaS server monitoring system. I wrote a cross-platform Node.js agent that runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows, pushing real-time metrics every 10 seconds via Redis pub/sub to live Socket.io dashboards. The platform covers CPU, memory, disk, GPU, security events, vulnerability scanning, PM2 app monitoring, log collection, and configurable alerts. Building a production SaaS end-to-end — from database schema design to Kubernetes deployment — was one of the most complete engineering experiences of my career.

chapters.title: ServerPulse — Launching a Live SaaS Product
chapters.content: I built and launched ServerPulse (serverpulse.in) — a real-time server analytics platform. From scratch I designed the architecture, wrote the backend, built the React frontend, and deployed it as a live product. ServerPulse tracks CPU, memory, disk, network, and process-level metrics across multiple servers in real time from a single dashboard. Shipping a live product that real users access was a completely different challenge from internal tools — it forced me to think about reliability, UX, and scalability simultaneously.

chapters.title: Embracing AI — AWS AI Practitioner
chapters.content: I became genuinely excited about the intersection of DevOps and AI. I earned the AWS Certified AI Practitioner certification and started building AI-augmented infrastructure tools — using AWS Bedrock and the Claude API for intelligent cost optimization, LLM-powered incident response bots, and ML-based anomaly detection in CloudWatch logs. I believe the next generation of platform engineering will be deeply AI-assisted, and I want to be at that frontier.

chapters.title: What Drives Me
chapters.content: I'm passionate about building systems that make engineers more productive and organisations more resilient. Whether it's a zero-downtime Kubernetes deployment, a cost optimisation that saves thousands of dollars a month, or an AI bot that resolves incidents before humans even notice — I care deeply about the impact of infrastructure on real people and real businesses. My goal is to grow into a world-class SRE and Platform Engineer who bridges DevOps and AI.
